subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Sheltering in Place
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Sheltering in Place.

hook.text: Sometimes the safest thing to do in a disaster isn't to flee—it's to stay exactly where you are.

buildup.text: Chemical spills, nuclear incidents, and severe storms may require sheltering in place.

discovery.text: Sealing windows with plastic sheeting and turning off ventilation reduces chemical exposure.

twist.text: Evacuating through a chemical cloud is more dangerous than staying inside a sealed room.

climax.text: Knowing when to shelter versus when to evacuate can mean the difference between safety and exposure.

punchline.text: Run or stay? The answer depends on what's outside. Know before it happens.

quiz.question: When is sheltering in place the safest option?
options: During chemical spills or nuclear incidents
